tools.name: list_libraries
tools.description: Returns INDEX.md contents from the current project and all registered libraries. Call this at the start of a task to discover existing utilities and avoid reimplementing functionality.

cwd.description: Current working directory of the Claude session

tools.name: get_design_doc
tools.description: Returns the full content of a specific design document. Call this when you need implementation details for functionality discovered via list_libraries.

library.description: Library name as shown in list_libraries response

tools.name: get_index_status
tools.description: Returns the status of INDEX.md compared to actual design doc files. Shows which docs are missing from INDEX.md and which INDEX entries are stale. Use this before updating INDEX.md to see what needs to be added or removed.

cwd.description: Current working directory of the Claude session

prompts.name: update_index
prompts.description: Guidelines and template for updating INDEX.md. Use this when you need to add new modules or clean up stale entries.

findings.title: 'list_libraries.cwd' silently accepts wrong type
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'list_libraries' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)
findings.description: The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.
findings.remediation: Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 3 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Protocol crash — JSON-RPC batch array caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 batch (5 requests as an array) caused the server to crash or disconnect. MCP does not define batch semantics; servers must reject arrays gracefully.
findings.evidence: Server disconnected after batch. Pre-crash response: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":73,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Library 'test' not found.\n\nMa
findings.remediation: Validate input type at the JSON-RPC layer: if the parsed value is a list, return -32600 (Invalid Request) immediately without further processing.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: Server accepts requests without jsonrpc field
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request missing the required 'jsonrpc' field. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, this field is mandatory and must equal '2.0'.
findings.remediation: Validate that the 'jsonrpc' field is present and equals '2.0' before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for non-conformant messages.
